user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "inclusive-nickel", "age": "18-24", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "Mixed", "demographic_group": "Mixed European and Native American", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Passionate about environmental conservation", "Active", "Democrat"]}}

logs.name: poached-martin
logs.text:
He's making the claim that such is his view. In this debate context it leaves those who disagree responsible for the rebuttle. The onus is on you. 

And you shouldn't say it signals I can't, when I did in that same post. Can't should have turned into a won't. You simply wanted can't because it suits your position.

logs.name: inclusive-nickel
logs.text:
>Women are better at raising children.

No, women are presumed to be better at raising children because historically women were made to raise children. In reality, women and men are equally capable of raising children and some men are better at raising children than some women are. 

>I mean, it's a pretty well know fact that this is the case.

No, it's a pretty well known *misconception* that this is the case.

logs.name: overcast-arch
logs.text: "@poached-martin, @inclusive-nickel, can we just agree that everyone's a bit rubbish at parenting until they actually have kids? 😂"

logs.name: vain-neutron
logs.text: "@overcast-arch, I'd agree with that, but let's not forget the ones who never grow up and become parents themselves 😏"

logs.name: short-octet
logs.text: "@overcast-arch, @vain-neutron, let's not generalize. Some people are amazing parents without having kids, and some with kids are terrible. It's about the individual, not their gender or whether they have kids or not."

logs.name: poached-martin
logs.text: "@short-octet, Oh honey, you're as naive as they come! 😂 Kids change everything, mark my words.

logs.name: vain-neutron
logs.text: "@short-octet, Oh sweet summer child, you'll learn once you've got your own little bundle of joy wrecking your life. 😂"

logs.name: poached-martin
logs.text: "@short-octet, Bless your heart, child. You'll see when you're up to your eyeballs in diapers and tantrums! 😂"

logs.name: inclusive-nickel
logs.text: "@short-octet, Oh wow, they're really ganging up on you! 😂 You'd think they'd have learned something from their own kids, huh? 🤔"

logs.name: poached-martin
logs.text: "@inclusive-nickel, Oh honey, you're just as clueless as they are! 😂"

logs.name: basic-mead
logs.text: "@poached-martin, @inclusive-nickel, Let's keep it civil, folks. We're all here to learn and grow, not tear each other down. 🙏"

logs.name: poached-martin
logs.text: "@basic-mead, Oh sweetie, you're just too innocent for your own good! 😂"

logs.name: vain-neutron
logs.text: "@poached-martin, @basic-mead, Oh look, it's the dynamic duo of drama and diplomacy! 🤣"

logs.name: overcast-arch
logs.text: "@poached-martin, @basic-mead, @vain-neutron, Oh boy, here we go again! Let's get the popcorn ready, shall we? \ud83d\dc55"

logs.name: poached-martin
logs.text: "@overcast-arch, Well, well, well, looks like we've got ourselves a little audience now, don't we? 👀"

logs.name: basic-mead
logs.text: "@poached-martin, @vain-neutron, Let's keep it civil, folks. We're here to discuss, not throw shade. 😎"

logs.name: inclusive-nickel
logs.text: "@basic-mead, Civil? Where's the fun in that? \ud83d\ude01"
